title: Annual Car CO2 Emissions Calculator | Hacé Cuentas
description: Calculate annual CO2 emissions from your car based on fuel consumption. Free tool updated for 2026.
intro: Efficient car maintenance and operation require calculations for fuel consumption, power, tire pressure, torque, and performance. Using the correct formulas prevents excessive fuel consumption and mechanical damage. Calculate CO2 emissions from fuel consumption—free and fast. Values are current as of 2026 and updated regularly to keep you informed.
keyTakeaway: Formula: **~2.3 kg CO2/L gasoline**. Direct application with your input values. Calculate your car's annual CO2 emissions from fuel consumption.

fields.label: Annual kilometers
fields.help: Total distance driven per year in kilometers.

explanation:
**Automotive calculations** are essential for car, motorcycle, and vehicle owners: fuel efficiency, maintenance costs, tire pressure, power conversions, and payback periods.

## How It's Calculated

This calculator computes CO2 emissions from fuel consumption by multiplying distance driven by the conversion factor (kg CO2 per liter of fuel). It verifies typical ranges and alerts you when values seem unreasonable.

## Important Notes

This calculation is an approximate reference. For critical decisions (financial, medical, legal), consult with a qualified professional. Values are current as of 2026 and reviewed regularly.

faq.q: What's the CO2 emission factor for gasoline?
faq.a: Approximately 2.31 kg CO2 per liter of gasoline, 2.68 kg per liter for diesel.

faq.q: How much CO2 does my car emit per year?
faq.a: It depends on your annual kilometers and fuel efficiency. Use this calculator to find your personalized result.

faq.q: How many trees offset car emissions?
faq.a: One tree absorbs approximately 22 kg of CO2 per year.

faq.q: Can I offset my car's carbon footprint?
faq.a: Yes, through voluntary carbon offset projects and reforestation initiatives.
Results are approximate using internationally validated standard formulas. For critical cases, consult with a professional.
Yes, completely free with no sign-up or registration required.
EV emissions depend on your country's electricity grid mix and energy sources.

faq.q: How does car sharing affect emissions?
faq.a: Divide total emissions by the number of occupants to calculate per-person impact.

faq.q: Do you store my personal data?
faq.a: No. All calculations happen in your browser. No data is sent to our servers.

sources.name: NIST — National Institute of Standards and Technology

dataUpdate.notes: Formula is fixed and does not require external data updates
